title: Rubus hirtus microgene crassus
intro: Rubus hirtus microgene crassus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a wild raspberry species native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its small size and robust thorns. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Rubus hirtus microgene crassus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of jams, jellies, and other value-added products.
